title: Salty Silky Norwegian Fish Stew
description: A traditional Norwegian fish stew made with cod, potatoes, and onions, simmered in a salty and slightly acidic broth. This dish is kid-friendly and gluten-free-friendly, making it a great option for families with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Rinse the cod fillets under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced potatoes and sliced carrots, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the cod pieces, white vinegar, sea salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the stew simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill if desired.
